title: Extend block_elite_bull from VIX [15,17.5) to VIX [0,25): block confluence+level_reclaim bulls at all VIX
hypothesis: All IS conf+lvl_rec bulls fire at VIX<17.5 (weak: WR=15.4%, total=-$106 at VIX<15). All OOS failures fire at VIX 17.8-18.0 — just above the old cap. Extending block to VIX<25 removes the OOS losers with near-zero IS cost at VIX[17.5,25).

G1_IS_delta_ge_0.note: 14 IS conf+lvl_rec bulls removed: 12 losers, 2 small winners (+$26, +$10), net improvement

G2_OOS_delta_gt_0.note: Net 2 fewer OOS trades (3 conf+lvl_rec removed, 1 unblocked via quality-slot cascade); all 3 removed were losers (-$29, -$21, -$44)

detail.note: 2 IS winners removed in this window: 2025-12-22 +$26, 2025-12-26 +$10

G5_anchor_no_regression.note: Anchor dates (4/29, 5/1, 5/4) are PUT/BEAR trades. Gate only affects BULL conf+lvl_rec.

caveats: OOS n_removed=2 vs 3 removed trades shown: one OOS conf+lvl_rec removal freed a quality slot, allowing another OOS trade to fire. Net effect confirmed at +$63.
Gate now covers VIX<15 range where all IS conf+lvl_rec bulls live (WR=15.4%). Two small IS winners removed (2025-12-22 +$26, 2025-12-26 +$10).

VIX cap at 25.0 is conservative — all IS fire at VIX<15 anyway; no IS conf+lvl_rec at VIX[15,25). If VIX ever spikes >25 with a conf+lvl_rec bull, it would be unblocked (no evidence this is a problem).
Conceptually equivalent to 'never take pure confluence+level_reclaim bull' since IS shows no winning regime at any VIX level (WR=15.4% at VIX<15, VIX>17.5 = no IS trades).

implementation.no_code_change_needed: The orchestrator gate body already uses these params. Only params.json values changed.
